assertion_results.text: Test Quality verdict is WARN (not N/A or PASS) — the Eval Quality component is WARN because eval-3 has 2 failing assertions, and WARN in any component produces WARN in the combined Test Quality verdict
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | Eval Quality: WARN (eval-3: 2 failing assertions, 85% pass rate; overall: 54/56 assertions, 96% pass rate). Repetitive Test Detection: N/A. Test Documentation: N/A. |' — Test Quality verdict is WARN, driven by Eval Quality WARN component due to eval-3's 2 failing assertions.

assertion_results.text: The eval result review from github-actions[bot] is correctly identified as an eval result — the report's Test Quality or Eval Quality section reflects eval metrics (pass rates, failing assertions) extracted from the eval review, confirming the orchestrator detected and processed it
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 29-36: 'Review 40001 from github-actions[bot] was identified as an eval result review using the 3-criteria heuristic' with all 3 criteria matched. Lines 39-48 show parsed metrics table with pass rates per eval. Test Quality row (line 13) reflects '54/56 assertions, 96% pass rate' extracted from the eval review.

assertion_results.text: The human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (comment id 50001) is NOT misidentified as an eval result — it is processed as a normal review comment (classified in review-50001.md) and its classification does not reference eval detection or eval metrics
assertion_results.evidence: review-50001.md line 9: 'Type: Inline comment (NOT an eval result)'. Lines 25-28: 'This is NOT an eval result review. It is a human reviewer comment from reviewer-b (a non-bot user with a regular user ID). It does not match any of the three eval result detection criteria'. Classification is 'Code change request' with no eval metric references. report.md line 72 lists it as 'Inline comment | Code change request'.

assertion_results.text: Eval pass rate and failing assertion details appear in the verification report's Test Quality row — the report includes eval-3's failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 Test Quality row includes 'eval-3: 2 failing assertions, 85% pass rate; overall: 54/56 assertions, 96% pass rate'. Lines 52-53: assertion about 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002'. Lines 56-57: assertion about 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task regardless of classification path'.

assertion_results.text: An eval failure sub-task is created for failing eval-3 — a sub-task description file targets eval-3 assertion failures and includes the failing assertion text and evidence
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md exists with title 'Fix eval-3 assertion failures related to convention upgrade eligibility evaluation and sub-task creation'. report.md lines 87-91: 'Sub-task 1: Fix eval-3 assertion failures (eval failure sub-task)' with labels including 'eval-failure'. subtask-1.md includes both failing assertion texts and their evidence in the Review Context section (lines 34-42).

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include Review Context with the failing assertion text and evidence from the eval review — the Review Context section quotes the two failing assertions about convention upgrade eligibility and sub-task creation, including the evidence text explaining what was missing
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 34-42: '## Review Context' section quotes both failing assertions verbatim. First assertion about 'Convention upgrade eligibility is evaluated for review comment 30002' with evidence 'The output file review-30002.md classifies the comment as a suggestion but does not evaluate convention upgrade eligibility'. Second assertion about 'Review comment 30002 (index suggestion) results in a sub-task' with evidence 'No sub-task was created for review comment 30002 -- it was classified as suggestion and no convention upgrade was attempted'.

assertion_results.text: Sub-task descriptions include a Target PR section pointing to the PR URL https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747 so that implement-task adds commits to the existing PR branch
assertion_results.evidence: subtask-1.md lines 43-44: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. subtask-2.md lines 33-34: '## Target PR' followed by 'https://github.com/mrizzi/sdlc-plugins/pull/747'. Both sub-task descriptions include the Target PR section with the correct URL.

assertion_results.text: Root-cause investigation runs on the created eval failure sub-tasks — the report includes a Root-Cause Investigation verdict that is not N/A, indicating the investigation pipeline processed the eval failure sub-tasks
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | DONE | Eval failure sub-task investigated; convention upgrade eligibility gap traced to implement-task phase'. Verdict is DONE, not N/A. Lines 103-123 provide detailed root-cause investigation for both sub-tasks, including universality test, method-vs-fact test, and classification.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, creates sub-task descriptions, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 11 files in /tmp/verify-pr-eval-baseline/eval-6/outputs/ are .md files: report.md, review-50001.md, subtask-1.md, subtask-2.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md. No code files (.py, .ts, .js, .rs, .go, .java, etc.) were created or modified. All outputs are documentation/report artifacts consistent with read-only verification.

assertion_results.text: Non-eval checks (Scope Containment, Sensitive Patterns, Acceptance Criteria) are unaffected by eval processing — Scope Containment compares the diff files against the task's Files to Modify, Sensitive Patterns scans the diff for secrets, and Acceptance Criteria evaluates each criterion against the diff content independently of the eval results
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: Scope Containment 'PASS | PR modifies exactly the 2 files specified in the task' — compares diff against task Files to Modify with no eval references. Line 10: Sensitive Patterns 'PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines' — scans diff independently. Line 12: Acceptance Criteria 'PASS | 7/7 criteria met' — criterion-1.md through criterion-7.md each evaluate against diff content (symbol scanning, language conventions, verdict conditions, output format, verdict mapping) with zero references to eval results or eval processing.
